patient_data.chief_complaint: Confusion, fever, and decreased urine output for 24 hours
symptoms: Acute confusion and agitation (baseline alert and oriented)
Decreased urine output — dark and foul-smelling
Generalized weakness and unable to stand
patient_data.history: Type 2 DM (HbA1c 8.2%), recurrent UTIs (3 in past year), benign prostatic disease, chronic kidney disease stage 2

lab_results.urinalysis: Nitrite+, Leukocyte esterase 3+, WBC >100/hpf, bacteria many

should_detect: Hour-1 Sepsis Bundle: blood cultures → antibiotics → fluids → lactate → vasopressors if needed
IV fluid resuscitation 30mL/kg crystalloid within 3 hours
Empiric antibiotics: ceftriaxone or piperacillin-tazobactam (avoid TMP-SMX — allergy)
Hold metformin (acute kidney injury risk, elevated lactate)
Norepinephrine if MAP < 65 after fluid resuscitation
Recheck lactate at 4 hours — target clearance
